successCriteria.description: src/modules/notification/notification.model.ts exists and exports Notification, NotificationType, NotificationStatus, CreateNotificationDto
src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exists and exports AuditLog, CreateAuditLogDto
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exists and exports IAuditRepository, AuditRepository
ARCHITECTURE.md updated to document Notification and AuditLog entities and NotificationType enum values

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces (INotificationRepository, IAuditRepository).
Audit records: All state-changing operations must write an audit record using the AuditRepository (the repository itself is the mechanism).
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing (DTOs will be validated when used in later phases).
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data in repository methods.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections; all async errors must be caught and handled appropriately.
outOfScope: Integration with other modules (leave, employee, policy, balance).
API routes, controllers, or service layer for notification or audit.
Database migration scripts or table creation.
Actual notification sending logic (email, push, etc.).
Audit logging implementation in other services (only the repository is created).
Phase 10: Wiring services, routes, and controllers.
